project.name: FeedbackFlow AI
project.description: Automated micro-SaaS to ingest, analyze, and triage real-world B2B/B2C public reviews using LLMs. Pulls in real customer feedback from a public source (GitHub Issues API), runs a multi-task LLM analysis on each item (sentiment, topics, severity, summary), stores the raw + structured results in Postgres, and presents a dashboard + inbox + detail UI for Product Managers and Customer Success teams to triage it.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Next.js dynamic route page that renders a single feedback item's detail view, fetching the record via Prisma and displaying its analysis, topics, and status selector.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Inbox listing page that parses search params for sentiment, status, topic, sort, and pagination filters, queries Prisma for feedback items and analyses, and renders a paginated, filterable table.

nodes.name: Badges.tsx
nodes.summary: Presentational React components rendering sentiment, severity, status, and topic badges with conditional Tailwind class names via the cn helper.

nodes.name: Filters.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side filter bar component that reads and updates URL search params for sentiment, severity, status, topic, sort, and pagination using Next.js router and searchParams hooks.

nodes.name: StatusSelect.tsx
nodes.summary: Client component providing a dropdown to change a feedback item's status, persisting the update via fetch to the PATCH endpoint and refreshing the router on success.

nodes.name: github.ts
nodes.summary: GitHub issues ingestion adapter that builds an Octokit client, paginates issues with rate-limit parsing and exponential backoff, and normalizes results into the internal feedback item shape.

nodes.name: llm.ts
nodes.summary: OpenAI analysis module that builds prompts from feedback items, calls chat completions, validates responses with a Zod schema, and supports batch analysis with retry/backoff.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Shared constant definitions for sentiment labels, topic taxonomy, and feedback statuses used across the inbox UI and ingestion pipeline.

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: Shared utility helpers including className concatenation, date formatting, text truncation, sleep, and exponential backoff delay used across UI and pipeline modules.

nodes.name: FeedbackDetailPage
nodes.summary: Server component that loads a single feedback item by id via Prisma, renders its metadata, analysis topics, and status selector, and triggers notFound when missing.

nodes.name: InboxPage
nodes.summary: Server component that validates search params, queries Prisma for paginated feedback items and analyses, aggregates topic options, and renders the filterable inbox table with pagination controls.

nodes.name: qs
nodes.summary: Helper that builds a URLSearchParams string from a base params object and override entries, supporting both array and scalar values.

nodes.name: SentimentBadge
nodes.summary: Renders a colored badge label for a feedback sentiment value using conditional Tailwind classes.

nodes.name: SeverityBadge
nodes.summary: Renders a severity score badge with color scaling based on numeric severity level.

nodes.name: StatusBadge
nodes.summary: Renders a status badge with color and label derived from a feedback status string.

nodes.name: TopicChip
nodes.summary: Renders a small chip label for a single analysis topic string.

nodes.name: Filters
nodes.summary: Client component rendering sentiment, severity, status, topic, sort, and pagination filter controls that sync state to URL search params via the Next.js router.

nodes.name: Toggle
nodes.summary: Small presentational toggle button used by the Filters component to show active/inactive filter state.

nodes.name: StatusSelect
nodes.summary: Client component dropdown that updates a feedback item's status via a PATCH fetch call, manages saving/error state, and refreshes the router on success.

nodes.name: parseRateLimit
nodes.summary: Parses GitHub rate-limit headers (remaining, limit, reset) from a response into a structured info object.

nodes.name: waitForRateLimit
nodes.summary: Sleeps until the GitHub rate-limit reset time when the remaining quota is exhausted, logging a warning before waiting.

nodes.name: fetchGitHubIssues
nodes.summary: Fetches recent GitHub issues via Octokit with pagination, rate-limit handling, and exponential backoff, normalizing each issue into the internal feedback item shape.

nodes.name: buildUserPrompt
nodes.summary: Constructs the user-facing prompt text for the LLM from a feedback item's source, author, title, and truncated raw content.

nodes.name: analyzeFeedback
nodes.summary: Sends a single feedback item to the OpenAI chat completions API, parses and validates the JSON response against the Zod AnalysisSchema, and retries with backoff on failure.

nodes.name: analyzeBatch
nodes.summary: Iterates a list of feedback items, calling analyzeFeedback for each and collecting successful analyses and failures into separate result arrays.

nodes.name: cn
nodes.summary: Concatenates truthy class name arguments into a single space-separated className string.

nodes.name: formatDate
nodes.summary: Formats an ISO date or Date object into a human-readable locale string, returning a placeholder for invalid input.

nodes.name: formatDay
nodes.summary: Formats an ISO date or Date object into a short locale date string, returning a placeholder for invalid input.

nodes.name: truncate
nodes.summary: Truncates a string to a maximum length and trims trailing whitespace.

nodes.name: sleep
nodes.summary: Returns a promise that resolves after the given number of milliseconds, used for rate-limit and backoff waits.

nodes.name: backoffDelay
nodes.summary: Computes an exponential backoff delay with jitter for a given attempt number, capped at a maximum milliseconds value.

nodes.name: seed.ts
nodes.summary: Database seeding script that creates a demo user and populates sample feedback items with analyses for local development and testing.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that hashes a demo password, upserts a seed user, and conditionally invokes sample feedback seeding.

nodes.name: seedSamples
nodes.summary: Populates the database with sample feedback items and associated analyses using Prisma upserts, generating timestamps across past days.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js catch-all route that wires the shared NextAuth authOptions into GET and POST handlers for the authentication endpoints.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Signup API endpoint that validates credentials with a Zod schema, checks for existing users, hashes passwords, and creates new user records.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Handles user registration by validating the request body, preventing duplicate emails, hashing the password with bcrypt, and persisting the new user via Prisma.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Dashboard analytics API endpoint that aggregates feedback analyses by sentiment, status, severity, and topic, plus a daily feedback volume time series.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Authenticates the session then returns aggregated dashboard metrics: sentiment/status/severity/topic distributions, recent feedback counts, and a 14-day daily volume series.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Feedback item API endpoints supporting fetching a single feedback item by id and patching its analysis (status, sentiment, severity, topics).

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Returns a single feedback item by id after verifying the user session via getServerSession.

nodes.name: PATCH
nodes.summary: Validates and applies partial updates to a feedback item's analysis (status, sentiment, severity, topics) using a Zod patch schema and Prisma.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Feedback list API endpoint with filtering by sentiment, status, and topic, plus sorting and pagination over feedback items and their analyses.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Authenticates the session then returns a paginated, filterable list of feedback items joined with analyses, validating filter values against project enum constants.

nodes.name: auth.ts
nodes.summary: Configures NextAuth authOptions with credentials provider, JWT session strategy, and an authorize callback that validates email/password against the Prisma user table.

nodes.name: prisma.ts
nodes.summary: Prisma client singleton that instantiates a single PrismaClient instance and reuses it in development to avoid exhausting database connections.

nodes.name: handler.ts
nodes.summary: AWS Lambda entry point that invokes the shared ingest pipeline and returns a JSON summary of the run, with error handling that surfaces failures to the Lambda runtime.

nodes.name: ingest.ts
nodes.summary: Command-line script that runs the ingest pipeline once and prints the result summary, exiting with a non-zero code on failure for CI integration.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js App Router POST handler that authenticates the session via NextAuth and triggers the ingest pipeline, returning JSON status responses.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js App Router webhook endpoint for Slack integration, exposing GET (status check) and POST (send a Slack message) handlers gated by session auth and a Slack-enabled feature flag.

nodes.name: ingest.ts
nodes.summary: Core ingest pipeline orchestrator that fetches raw feedback items from a source, deduplicates against existing records, batches them through LLM analysis, persists results in a Prisma transaction, and notifies Slack on high-severity findings.

nodes.name: slack.ts
nodes.summary: Slack notification helper module providing a feature-flag check, a generic message sender via the Slack incoming webhook, and a high-severity feedback notifier that formats topics and sentiment for alerting.

nodes.name: handler
nodes.summary: AWS Lambda handler that runs the ingest pipeline, logs progress, and returns a structured JSON response with counts and any failures.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Authenticates the request session and invokes the ingest pipeline, returning a JSON response with the run result or an error status.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Status-check handler that verifies session auth and reports whether the Slack integration is enabled.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Authenticates the session, checks the Slack feature flag, and forwards a payload to sendSlackMessage, returning a JSON success or error response.

nodes.name: runIngest
nodes.summary: Main ingest pipeline function that fetches raw feedback, deduplicates against the database, batches items through LLM analysis, atomically persists items and analyses, and triggers Slack alerts for high-severity results.

nodes.name: persistLog
nodes.summary: Persists an ingest run log entry to the database via Prisma, swallowing and logging errors so logging failures never break the pipeline.

nodes.name: isSlackEnabled
nodes.summary: Returns whether Slack notifications are enabled based on the presence of the Slack webhook URL environment variable.

nodes.name: sendSlackMessage
nodes.summary: Posts an arbitrary payload to the configured Slack incoming webhook URL via fetch and returns a success boolean with error logging on failure.

nodes.name: notifyHighSeverity
nodes.summary: Formats a high-severity feedback alert (topics and sentiment) and sends it to Slack when the integration is enabled.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Server-rendered dashboard page that aggregates feedback analysis metrics (sentiment, severity, topic distribution, trend) via Prisma and renders KPI cards, Recharts visualizations, and a high-severity feedback list.

nodes.name: getDashboardData
nodes.summary: Server-side data loader that queries Prisma for sentiment/severity groupings, topic counts, daily sentiment trends, high-severity items, and ingest status to feed the dashboard.

nodes.name: DashboardPage
nodes.summary: Default-exported React server component that authenticates the session, loads dashboard data, and renders KPI cards, chart components, and a high-severity feedback list.

nodes.name: KpiCard
nodes.summary: Presentational helper component rendering a labeled KPI metric card with an accent color.

nodes.name: SentimentDonutChart.tsx
nodes.summary: Recharts-based donut chart component visualizing the distribution of positive, neutral, and negative sentiment counts.

nodes.name: SentimentDonutChart
nodes.summary: Exported React component that normalizes sentiment group data into positive/neutral/negative series and renders a Recharts PieChart with a donut layout and legend.

nodes.name: SentimentTrendChart.tsx
nodes.summary: Recharts line chart component displaying daily sentiment trend counts over time for the dashboard.

nodes.name: SentimentTrendChart
nodes.summary: Exported React component that slices trend data and renders a Recharts line chart of daily sentiment counts.

nodes.name: SeverityChart.tsx
nodes.summary: Recharts bar chart component visualizing feedback counts grouped by severity level (1-5) for the dashboard.

nodes.name: SeverityChart
nodes.summary: Exported React component that normalizes severity group data across levels 1-5 and renders a Recharts bar chart with colored bars and a legend.

nodes.name: TopicDistributionChart.tsx
nodes.summary: Recharts bar chart component showing the distribution of feedback items across detected topics for the dashboard.

nodes.name: TopicDistributionChart
nodes.summary: Exported React component that slices topic count data and renders a Recharts horizontal bar chart of topic distribution.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template of all runtime environment variables for FeedbackFlow AI, including NextAuth secrets, the Postgres DATABASE_URL, OpenAI API key and model, GitHub Issues source repo and token, ingest tuning knobs, and optional Slack/SMTP digest settings.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive project documentation covering features, tech stack, architecture, local quick-start, data model, LLM output format, project structure, AWS deployment, security, and acceptance-criteria mapping for FeedbackFlow AI.

nodes.name: amplify.yml
nodes.summary: AWS Amplify build specification for the Next.js frontend, running npm ci, prisma generate, and next build, then publishing the .next directory as build artifacts with node_modules and .next/cache caching.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Local development Docker Compose file defining a single postgres:16-alpine service (feedbackflow-db) on port 5432 with a persistent volume and a pg_isready healthcheck, used by the npm run db:up script.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Root package manifest for the feedbackflow-ai Next.js 14 app, declaring scripts for dev/build/lint/typecheck, Prisma workflows, local db compose control, seed and ingest runners, Lambda bundling, and SAM validate/build/deploy, plus all runtime and dev dependencies.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the project targeting ES2022 with strict mode, bundler module resolution, JSX preserve, incremental builds, the Next.js plugin, and an @/* path alias mapping to ./src/*.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore-pattern file listing paths excluded from the understand-anything codebase analysis pipeline.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: AWS deployment guide covering backend provisioning (RDS, Lambda, EventBridge), schema migration, frontend Amplify deployment, and cost notes.

nodes.name: template.yaml
nodes.summary: AWS SAM/CloudFormation template provisioning VPC networking, an RDS Postgres instance, Secrets Manager credentials, and an EventBridge-scheduled Lambda ingest function.

nodes.name: next.config.mjs
nodes.summary: Next.js configuration enabling React strict mode and standalone output builds for containerized deployment.

nodes.name: postcss.config.js
nodes.summary: PostCSS configuration registering Tailwind CSS and Autoprefixer plugins for the project's styling pipeline.

nodes.name: migration.sql
nodes.summary: Initial Prisma migration creating the core PostgreSQL tables (users, feedback_items, feedback_analyses, ingest_logs) with unique constraints and query indexes.

nodes.name: users
nodes.summary: Auth users table storing email, hashed password, name, and audit timestamps with a unique email constraint.

nodes.name: feedback_items
nodes.summary: Ingested raw feedback table keyed by source and external id for idempotent ingestion, with indexes on source and original timestamp.

nodes.name: feedback_analyses
nodes.summary: LLM analysis table linked 1:1 to feedback items via foreign key, storing sentiment, topics, severity score, summary, and triage status.

nodes.name: ingest_logs
nodes.summary: Observability log table recording each cron ingest run's source, status, item counts, and error details.

nodes.name: migration_lock.toml
nodes.summary: Prisma migration lock file pinning the database provider to postgresql to prevent cross-provider migrations.

nodes.name: schema.prisma
nodes.summary: Prisma schema defining the PostgreSQL data model with User, FeedbackItem, FeedbackAnalysis, and IngestLog models plus enums for triage and run statuses.

nodes.name: build-lambda.ts
nodes.summary: Build script that compiles the Lambda handler and bundles the Prisma client into the AWS Lambda deployment package.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that runs the TypeScript build, copies the generated Prisma client into the Lambda output directory, and logs progress.

nodes.name: layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Authenticated app route-group layout that gates access via a server-side session check and renders the Navbar around nested pages.

nodes.name: AppLayout
nodes.summary: Server component that verifies the user session with getServerSession, redirecting unauthenticated visitors to login, then renders the Navbar and children.

nodes.name: globals.css
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet with Tailwind CSS directives and base layer customizations for the application theme.

nodes.name: layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Root Next.js App Router layout that sets HTML metadata and wraps the application in the session Providers component.

nodes.name: RootLayout
nodes.summary: Root layout component rendering the HTML shell, metadata, and wrapping children in the NextAuth Providers wrapper.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Login page rendering a credentials sign-in form with email/password fields, loading and error states, and post-login redirect handling.

nodes.name: LoginPage
nodes.summary: Login route page component that renders the LoginForm client component.

nodes.name: LoginForm
nodes.summary: Client component managing email/password state and submitting credentials via NextAuth signIn, handling errors and redirecting to the callback URL on success.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Root home page that immediately redirects visitors to the authenticated dashboard route.

nodes.name: Home
nodes.summary: Home page component that redirects the user to the dashboard on render.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Signup page rendering a registration form that posts to the signup API, signs the new user in, and redirects to the dashboard.

nodes.name: SignupPage
nodes.summary: Client component managing name/email/password state, submitting the registration request via fetch, then signing in and redirecting to the dashboard on success.

nodes.name: Navbar.tsx
nodes.summary: Navigation bar component showing active-route links and a sign-out button, adapting to the current session state.

nodes.name: Navbar
nodes.summary: Client component rendering navigation links with active-route highlighting via the cn utility and a sign-out action using the current session.

nodes.name: Providers.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side wrapper component that mounts the NextAuth SessionProvider around the React tree.

nodes.name: Providers
nodes.summary: Wrapper component that renders the NextAuth SessionProvider around its children.

nodes.name: middleware.ts
nodes.summary: NextAuth middleware that protects authenticated routes by validating the session token on each request.

nodes.name: next-auth.d.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript module augmentation extending the NextAuth session and JWT types with a custom userId field.

nodes.name: tailwind.config.ts
nodes.summary: Tailwind CSS configuration defining the content scan paths, theme extensions, and the project's design tokens.

nodes.name: tsconfig.tsbuildinfo
nodes.summary: Auto-generated TypeScript incremental build cache storing compiler state for faster rebuilds.

layers.name: UI Layer
layers.description: Next.js App Router pages, root and authenticated layouts, and global styles that form the presentation and routing shell of the application (excluding API route handlers).

layers.name: Component Layer
layers.description: Reusable React UI components including navigation, providers, badges, filters, status controls, and Recharts-based dashboard visualizations.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: Next.js App Router API route handlers exposing REST endpoints for authentication, dashboard analytics, feedback CRUD, ingest triggering, and Slack webhooks.

layers.name: Service Layer
layers.description: Core business logic and domain services in src/lib: the ingest pipeline orchestrator, LLM analysis, GitHub ingestion adapter, Slack notifications, NextAuth configuration, the Prisma client singleton, and request-protection middleware.

layers.name: Shared Utility & Types Layer
layers.description: Foundational shared helpers and type definitions with high fan-in used across UI, API, and service layers: className/date/format/backoff utilities, sentiment/topic/status constants, and NextAuth type augmentations.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: Database schema and persistence definitions: the Prisma schema, initial PostgreSQL migration SQL, individual table definitions, and the migration lock file.

layers.name: Infrastructure & Operations Layer
layers.description: Deployment and operational tooling: AWS SAM/CloudFormation template, the Lambda ingest handler, Docker Compose for local Postgres, the Amplify build spec, and build/seed/ingest CLI scripts.

layers.name: Configuration Layer
layers.description: Project-level configuration and build settings: package manifest, TypeScript compiler config, environment variable template, Next.js/PostCSS/Tailwind configs, the incremental build cache, and analysis ignore patterns.

layers.name: Documentation Layer
layers.description: Project documentation including the root README covering architecture and deployment, and the AWS deployment guide.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to grasp what FeedbackFlow AI is and why it exists. It is an automated micro-SaaS that ingests real customer feedback from GitHub Issues, runs multi-task LLM analysis (sentiment, topics, severity, summary) on each item, stores raw + structured results in Postgres, and exposes a dashboard, inbox, and detail UI for triage. The README maps features to architecture and gives you the vocabulary for the rest of the tour.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: The Next.js App Router boots from the root layout, which sets HTML metadata and wraps the whole tree in the SessionProvider. The root page simply redirects visitors to the authenticated dashboard. Together these two files define the runtime shell: every route renders inside the root layout, and unauthenticated traffic is bounced toward login before reaching the app.

tour.title: Authentication & Route Protection
tour.description: Security is enforced at two levels. The middleware validates the NextAuth session token on every request to protected paths, while the auth library configures the credentials provider with a JWT session strategy and an authorize callback that checks email/password against the Prisma user table. The catch-all NextAuth route wires these options into the auth endpoints, and the signup route hashes passwords and creates users. This is the gate everything behind it depends on.

tour.title: Data Model & Schema
tour.description: The Prisma schema is the single source of truth for the Postgres data model, defining User, FeedbackItem, FeedbackAnalysis, and IngestLog models plus enums for triage and run statuses. The initial migration SQL creates the corresponding tables with unique constraints and query indexes. The feedback_items table is keyed by source + external id for idempotent ingestion, and feedback_analyses links 1:1 to it via a foreign key storing the LLM results. This schema is the contract every other layer reads and writes.

tour.title: Shared Foundation: Client, Types & Utilities
tour.description: Three small files have the highest fan-in in the whole codebase because nearly every other module imports them. The Prisma client singleton reuses one connection in development to avoid exhausting the pool. The shared types module defines sentiment labels, the topic taxonomy, and triage statuses used by both the UI and the pipeline. The utils module provides className concatenation, date formatting, truncation, and the sleep/backoff helpers the ingestion and LLM modules rely on for reliability.

tour.title: Ingest Pipeline Orchestrator
tour.description: The ingest module is the heart of the product: its runIngest function fetches raw feedback from a source, deduplicates against existing records by source + external id, batches items through LLM analysis, persists raw + analysis rows in a Prisma transaction, logs the run to ingest_logs, and notifies Slack on high-severity findings. It ties together the github, llm, prisma, and slack modules introduced next, and is the single function invoked by the CLI, the API route, and the Lambda handler.

tour.title: GitHub Issues Ingestion Adapter
tour.description: The github module is the real-world data source. It builds an Octokit client, paginates issues from a configurable public repo via the GitHub Issues REST API, parses rate-limit headers, and applies exponential backoff with the shared sleep/backoff helpers before normalizing each issue into the internal feedback item shape. This is what makes the pipeline ingest genuine customer feedback rather than seeded demo data.

tour.title: LLM Analysis with Structured Output
tour.description: The llm module turns raw text into structured insight. It builds a prompt from each feedback item, calls OpenAI chat completions, and validates the response against a Zod schema enforcing sentiment, topics, severity, and summary fields. analyzeBatch iterates with retry/backoff so a single bad response does not abort the whole run. This is where the README's multi-task analysis promise is actually fulfilled.

tour.title: Slack Notifications
tour.description: The slack module provides the bonus alerting layer. A feature-flag check gates whether Slack is enabled, a generic sender posts to the incoming webhook, and notifyHighSeverity formats topics and sentiment for items with severity >= 4. The ingest orchestrator calls this after persisting results, closing the loop from raw GitHub issue to a PM's Slack channel.

tour.title: API Routes
tour.description: The App Router API layer exposes REST endpoints over the service and data layers. The dashboard route aggregates analyses by sentiment, status, severity, and topic plus a daily volume time series. The feedback list and detail routes provide filtering, sorting, pagination, and PATCH-based triage updates. The ingest route authenticates the session and triggers runIngest on demand. Each route imports the high-fan-in auth and prisma modules from earlier steps.

tour.title: Dashboard & Inbox UI
tour.description: The UI is where Product Managers and Customer Success teams actually triage. The dashboard page server-renders KPI cards and four Recharts visualizations (sentiment donut, sentiment trend, severity bar, topic distribution) plus a high-severity list. The inbox page parses URL search params into filters and renders a paginated, sortable table. The detail page shows raw content alongside the AI analysis and a status selector. Reusable Badges, Filters, and StatusSelect components keep the presentation consistent.

tour.title: Infrastructure & Deployment
tour.description: The tour closes with how the app runs in production. The AWS SAM template provisions VPC networking, an RDS Postgres instance, Secrets Manager credentials, and an EventBridge-scheduled Lambda that invokes the same runIngest function on a daily cron. The Lambda handler imports the shared ingest module, and the build-lambda script bundles the Prisma client into the deployment package. Locally, docker-compose spins up Postgres, and the Amplify build spec deploys the Next.js frontend. The same ingest pipeline thus runs from npm, an API route, or Lambda without code changes.
